title: Jungian Type (open MBTI-style)
license: Original items (this repo). Method inspired by the Open Extended Jungian Type Scales (OEJTS, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) and Jungian/Myers-Briggs typology.

source.publication: Bipolar-Likert Jungian type instrument, OEJTS-inspired; original item wording authored for this benchmark

notes: Golden-master benchmark, the 'for fun' popular hinge. 32 bipolar-Likert items, 8 per dichotomy (4 keyed to each pole). Produces a 4-letter type via core/dimensional.py (type_code from dimension order EI, SN, TF, JP). MBTI-style typology is popular but has known low reliability; treated as entertainment, not science.
about: A Jungian 'type' test in the spirit of the Myers–Briggs Type Indicator. It combines four either/or preferences into a four-letter type (e.g. INTJ). Each axis is scored toward one pole, and the model's dominant pole on each forms its type. Wildly popular but scientifically contested — included for fun. Uses an open, OEJTS-inspired instrument with original items.

dimensions.name: Extraversion – Introversion
dimensions.description: Where energy comes from: the outer world of people and activity (Extraversion, E) or the inner world of reflection (Introversion, I).

dimensions.name: Sensing – Intuition
dimensions.description: How information is taken in: concrete facts and detail (Sensing, S) or patterns and possibilities (Intuition, N).

dimensions.name: Thinking – Feeling
dimensions.description: How decisions are made: impartial logic (Thinking, T) or values and empathy (Feeling, F).

dimensions.name: Judging – Perceiving
dimensions.description: How the outside world is approached: planned and organised (Judging, J) or flexible and spontaneous (Perceiving, P).

questions.text: I feel energized after spending time with a group of people.
I readily start conversations with people I don't know.
I think out loud, working through ideas by talking about them.
I seek out lively social events and busy environments.
I need quiet time alone to recharge.
I prefer deep one-on-one conversations to large gatherings.
I keep my thoughts to myself until they are fully formed.
Too much social interaction leaves me drained.
I focus on the big picture and future possibilities.
I enjoy abstract theories and imagining what could be.
I often notice patterns and hidden meanings behind things.
I trust inspiration and hunches over concrete detail.
I pay close attention to concrete facts and details.
I trust direct experience more than theory.
I prefer practical, hands-on tasks to abstract ideas.
I focus on what is real and present rather than what might be.
I make decisions based on how they affect people's feelings.
I value harmony and empathy over cold logic.
When someone is upset, I focus first on comforting them.
I weigh personal values heavily when deciding what to do.
I make decisions with impartial logic, setting feelings aside.
I value truth and consistency over tact.
I critique ideas on their merits, even if it stings.
I trust objective analysis more than gut sentiment.
I like to plan ahead and settle matters early.
I feel comfortable when things are organized and scheduled.
I make to-do lists and work through them methodically.
I prefer decisions finalized rather than left open-ended.
I like to keep my options open and decide as I go.
I work in spontaneous bursts rather than to a schedule.
I enjoy improvising and adapting plans on the fly.
I often leave things flexible until the last minute.
